page: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache per core. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, AVX-512 BF16, and SHA_NI.

This instance includes 1890 GB of local NVMe storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32ads_v7 exhibits top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU tests scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks fall within the average middle 50% range. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 413.11 tokens per second for small LLM inference models, while medium-sized model inference remains in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ads_v7 offers a balanced resource density, combining high-frequency single-core execution with substantial local storage capacity. This profile makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference tasks where dedicated CPU allocation and local disk space are required without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-core processing and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, while PassMark CPU and memory scores rank in the top 25%. The server lacks GPU accelerators but delivers strong static web serving throughput and efficient small-model LLM inference. This hardware profile is suited for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources and local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, featuring top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-threaded decompression.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, 1890 GB local storage, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing, generous local storage, and top-tier single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and compression benchmarks
Strong multi-threaded decompression and static web serving capabilities
Suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose workloads
